description_long: This triumphal arch is constructed from large, roughly hewn stone blocks, giving it a robust and ancient appearance. The structure features two thick vertical piers supporting a horizontal entablature, with a single central passageway beneath. The upper section is adorned with a band of slightly protruding stones, suggesting a decorative cornice or frieze. The surface texture is rugged and weathered, with visible irregularities and color variations typical of aged stone. There are no visible sculptural reliefs or ornate carvings, emphasizing a utilitarian and monumental style. The archway is wide and tall enough to allow passage for people and small vehicles, making it a prominent feature in an urban landscape.
description: A robust stone triumphal arch with two thick piers and a single central passage, featuring a rugged, weathered surface and a simple decorative band near the top.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the arch, the two piers, and the decorative band at the top.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the structure and the rough stone texture along the piers.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with visible stonework and the central passageway.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the depth and rugged stone surface.
